title: Creating a SAX Event Handler

Creating a SAX Event Handler

Creating a SAX Event Handler

Before starting to parse XML documents using the UXMLREADER component, you need to implement the operations that handle parser events, such as starting and ending the document and elements, and error handling.

You can choose to implement some operations, and not implement others, thus ignoring these specific events. For example, to count all start tags in an XML document, you only need to implement a STARTELEMENT operation that increments the number of tags by one, and a STARTDOCUMENT operation in which you set the number of tags to zero.

To create a SAX handler:

  1. Create a component that implements at least one SAX callback operation.

    These operations are activated by the PARSE and PARSEFILE operations of the UXMLREADER component.

    If you want to enable namespace support for the handler, you can include a STARTPREFIXMAPPING or ENDPREFIXMAPPING operation. For example, if you have an existing 4GL component with callback operations for UXMLREADER, and you wish to enable namespace support for this component without changing any code to process URI’s, prefixes, or unqualified names, you can add an STARTPREFIXMAPPING or ENDPREFIXMAPPING operation that does nothing.

    You can also enable namespace support in the STARTELEMENT or ENDELEMENT operations.

  2. Optionally, implement error handling operations to handle invalid data and other errors.

    Data read by the parser is expected to be legal—either there is no DTD defined for the data, or if there is a DTD, the tags, attributes and character data conform to the DTD. If this is not the case, the appropriate operation is activated, if present.

    • For warnings, the WARNING operation is activated.
    • If the error is deemed recoverable by the SAX parser, the ERROR operation is activated; otherwise, the FATALERROR operation is activated.
    • If there is no ERROR operation, recoverable errors are silently recovered from and ignored.
    • After a fatal error occurs, no further processing of the XML file is done and the PARSE or PARSEFILE operation returns a negative value.
    Number Error Returned by Description
    –57 <UACTERR_NO_INSTANCE> PARSE

    PARSEFILE

    No handler instance set

    If you invoke the PARSE or PARSEFILE operation without having set a handler instance, they will return this error and will not parse anything.
    –59 <UACTERR_NO_OPERATION> SETHANDLER No callback operation can be found.

    A valid handler component must implement at least one of the supported callback operations. Otherwise, SETHANDLER returns this error
    –151 <UACTERR_EXCEPTION_THROWN> PARSE

    PARSEFILE

    		A fatal error has occurred but there is no FATALERROR operation implemented.

    A FATALERROR operation is available but it returns nothing or a value >= 0

    A fatal error is deemed unrecoverable, by the SAX parser

To parse XML documents, you can instantiate and then activate the handler.

Related Links
  • SAX Callback Operations

    SAX callback operations are APIs that can be implemented on a user-defined SAX event handler. They enable you to use the parser information to manipulate and analyze XML data, and handle any errors that may be encountered.
